justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Timeout mismatch clearly identified with exact arithmetic (200+150+800=1150ms > 500ms). (2) Orphaned downstream request consequence thoroughly described — upstream abandons at 500ms while downstream calls continue for up to 1000ms, wasting resources. (3) Outbound > inbound timeout relationship explicitly called out across all three services. (4) Multiple remediation options provided: parallelization, reducing outbound timeouts, partial enrichment fallback, async enrichment, increasing upstream timeout. (5) Verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Exceptional depth on timeout budget analysis — not just identifying the mismatch but computing that even a single InventoryService p99 (800ms) alone exceeds the 500ms budget, making parallelization insufficient without additional changes.
Strong cascading failure analysis: thread pool exhaustion from slow downstreams, orphaned requests consuming capacity, health check coupling risk.
Correctly advises against adding retries in the current architecture — recognizes retries would compound the problem before the timeout budget is fixed.
Observability gaps are specific and actionable — per-service latency histograms, timeout-specific counters, in-flight request gauges, upstream-abandoned request metrics.
Escalations are well-routed: architectural decision to MJ, implementation work to Shaq.
Context cancellation propagation identified as a key missing piece — the mechanism by which orphaned requests should be stopped.
